title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Since its (0) INVENTION, photography has changed the way we document history. It allows for the (17) of moments that would otherwise be forgotten. For many, taking photos is an (18) of their unique artistic vision. Modern cameras are (19) easy to use, making photography accessible to everyone. However, being a (20) requires a deep understanding of light and composition. (21) must often wait for hours to capture the perfect shot. This (22) is what separates a snapshot from a masterpiece. The (23) of social media has further increased the (24) of visual arts in our daily lives. Today, images circulate faster than ever, shaping our perceptions of world events and personal stories alike. A single photograph can spark a global conversation or inspire a change in policy, proving that the lens is a truly powerful tool in the modern digital age.

example.explanation: Noun required after the possessive 'its'; INVENT → INVENTION.

questions.explanation: Adverb required to modify the adjective 'easy'; SURPRISE → SURPRISINGLY.
Noun for a person/role required; PROFESSION → PROFESSIONAL.
Plural noun for people required; PHOTOGRAPH → PHOTOGRAPHERS.
Noun required as the subject; PATIENT → PATIENCE.
Noun required after the definite article; VISIBLE → VISIBILITY.
